claims.text: In the space of just three weeks, the UN Human Rights Office documented 15 drone strikes on El Obeid, which killed at least 45 civilians. El Obeid has more than half a million residents, together with at least 100,000 internally displaced persons, and the number of newly displaced people across Kordofan has risen by nearly two-thirds in only three months.

G7 Foreign Ministers' joint statement on El Obeid, Sudan (14 July 2026): condemns ongoing RSF drone strikes on El Obeid and obstruction of humanitarian access, references the precedent of atrocities in El Fasher, and calls for an immediate ceasefire and compliance with the Jeddah Declaration to protect civilians.

NRC report (posted 7 July 2026) on El Obeid: families are dying from drone strikes and from hunger as the siege continues, with an aid convoy struck the preceding week; essential services and food access are collapsing further as the blockade persists.

UN News (17 July 2026): the UN human rights chief warned that civilians in El Obeid "have been under siege for 18 months" and face continuous drone strikes, with more than 100,000 displaced people sheltering in camps in the city. WFP is providing food assistance to over 100,000 people in those camps, including nutrition support for 17,000 children, but is distributing only a reduced ration that recipients are sharing with other families. Almost 20 million people in Sudan are now classified as acutely food insecure, and more than 14 million people have been uprooted nationwide since the conflict began.

description: Rapid Support Forces (RSF) paramilitary forces have besieged El Obeid, the capital of North Kordofan state, subjecting the city of more than half a million residents to relentless drone strikes and blockade conditions. The United Nations Human Rights Office documented 15 drone strikes on the city and surrounding areas in three weeks, and UN officials have warned of an imminent risk of mass atrocities similar to those already documented in El Fasher, Darfur. As of mid-July 2026, the UN human rights chief describes the siege as having lasted 18 months, with WFP forced to distribute reduced rations to over 100,000 people sheltering in city camps. G7 foreign ministers condemned the siege on 14 July 2026, and the UN Human Rights Council launched a Fact-Finding Mission inquiry into the situation.

impact.summary: The UN Human Rights Office documented 15 drone strikes on El Obeid and surrounding areas over three weeks that killed at least 45 civilians; no allowlisted source has published an updated aggregate casualty count since that initial 3 July UN report. As of a UN News report of 17 July 2026, the UN human rights chief describes civilians as having been "under siege for 18 months" amid continuous drone strikes, with more than 100,000 displaced people sheltering in city camps where WFP is distributing only reduced food rations. El Obeid has more than half a million residents. G7 foreign ministers (14 July 2026) have urged an immediate ceasefire; UN officials continue to warn of the risk of large-scale atrocities.

event.scale: City under siege; UN human-rights atrocity-risk warning

name: Siege of El Obeid — North Kordofan, Sudan
